MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
CmdTools (C++)

Magewell provides a collection of command line tools.

  1. AudioCapture Example C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to capture LPCM audio data and record it into a wav file based on Pro/Eco Capture Family Universal Functions. It is supported by the Pro Capture Family and Eco Capture Family.

  2. CaptureByInput C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to capture video data at the input signal frame rate and resolution and create a snapshot based on Pro Capture Family Functions. The video capturing is triggered by a message notification. Compared with CaptureByTimer, the frame rate here is fixed according to your input signal, which ensures the application’s stability.

  3. CaptureByField C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to capture video data based on Pro Capture Family Functions. If interlaced signal is input, it captures by field. This is only supported by the Pro Capture Family.

  4. CaptureByTimer C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to capture video data at a customize signal frame rate and create a snapshot. The video capturing is triggered by a timer based on Pro Capture Family Functions. The interval between any two frames in this routine is fixed, that is, the frame rate is unchanged. However, the time interval between two frames can change according to your needs.

  5. GetANCPacket C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to get the ANC (Ancillary) data of specific channels based on Pro/Eco Capture Family Universal Functions. In addition, ANC data can be obtained only with SDI signals. It is supported by the Pro Capture Family and Eco Capture Family.

  6. HDMIInfoFrame C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to get the HDMI InfoFrame. It is supported by the Pro, Eco and USB Gen2/Plus Capture Families which support HDMI signals.

  7. InputSignal C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to get the detailed format of input signal, including the status of specific channels, the detailed timings of input signal, the sampling and the format of an audio signal. We strongly recommend that developers study this example thoroughly. It is supported by the Pro, Eco and USB Gen2/Plus Capture Families.

  8. InputSource C++

    It demonstrates how to use Mangewell APIs to get the type and number of input sources. It is supported by the Pro, Eco and USB Gen2/Plus Capture Families.

  9. NvidiaCodec C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to capture videos and then encode videos in H.264 using Nvidia GPU. We recommended that developers refer to this example if they require high coding performance. It is supported by the Pro Capture Family.

  10. ReadWriteEDID C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to upload the EDID information from a file or export the EDID of specified channel to a bin file. It is supported by the Pro, Eco and USB Gen2/Plus Capture Families that support HDMI or VGA signals.

  11. USBDeviceDetect C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to detect the hot-plug status of USB capture devices. It is supported by the USB Gen2/Plus Capture Family.

  12. Mp4Repair C++

    It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to repair corrupted MP4/MOV files recorded using MP4 .

  13. board_id_capture

    It demonstrates how to access your device by using board_id and channel_id.

  14. color_conditioning

    It demonstrates how to adjust the contrast, brightness, saturation and chroma, and generate 5 pictures, which have different contrast, brightness, saturation and chroma by using MWCapture SDKv3 Pro Capture Functions.

  15. color_conditioning2

    It demonstrates how to adjust the contrast, brightness, saturation and chroma, and generate 5 pictures, which have different contrast, brightness, saturation and chroma by using MWCapture SDKv3 Universal Functions.

  16. crop_capture

    It demonstrates how to crop pictures and generate 5 pictures which are cropped in different ways.

  17. gpu_encode

    It demonstrates that GPU hardware acceleration the encoding of the captured video into H264 and h265 streams, and saves them to gpuenc.h264 or gpuenc.h265.

  18. low_latency

    It demonstrates the process of low latency capture, and comparison of the low-latency with normal capture.

  19. mp4_muxer

    It demonstrates the process of MP4 packaging and generation of h264_aac_cc608.mp4 file.
